title: T-124 — packages/e-sign — CFR 21 Part 11 dual-sign + server-verified PIN primitive (used by quality/production)
prompt:
# T-124 — packages/e-sign — CFR 21 Part 11 e-sign primitive

PRD: docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md §11 Audit log (FDA 21 CFR Part 11 — "electronic records — when a US tenant requires it, retention_class='security' extends to 10 years"); 09-QUALITY-PRD.md §5 Compliance + translation-notes-quality.md ("Build a single shared <ESignBlock> server component")
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Provide `packages/e-sign` (`@monopilot/e-sign`) — a small package implementing the dual-sign + server-verified PIN contract used by 09-Quality (haccp/ccp/incident/complaint sign-offs), 08-Production (WO release, dual-sign destructive actions), 03-Technical (FA release approval), and 10-Finance (cost approval ≥20%). Translation-notes-quality.md flags this as the canonical CFR 21 Part 11 primitive but no atomic task owns it (auditor B §3 Top-5 translation-rules gap).

This task delivers:
- A typed `signEvent({ subject, signerUserId, pin, intent, reason }) → ESignReceipt`,
- A dual-sign helper `dualSign({ subject, primarySignerUserId, primaryPin, secondarySignerUserId, secondaryPin, intent, reason })` that enforces SoD (the two signers MUST be different users),
- PIN verification calls existing `verifyPin` from `packages/auth` (T-016) — never plaintext, argon2id-hashed, server-only.
- Audit-event write per signature with `retention_class='security'` (10 years for US tenants per F-U5 / PRD §11).
- A replay-attack guard: each signature carries a server-generated nonce + `subject_hash` (sha256 of canonical-stringified subject) — re-submitting the same `{nonce, subject_hash}` is rejected with `EReplayError`.

## Implementation contract
1. Create `packages/e-sign/` workspace package. Deps: `@monopilot/auth` (workspace, for verifyPin), `@monopilot/db` (workspace), `argon2`, `zod`. Dev: vitest.
2. `packages/e-sign/src/types.ts` exports:
   - `ESignIntent = 'qa.hold.release' | 'qa.ncr.close' | 'qa.haccp.ccp.deviation' | 'prod.wo.release' | 'prod.consumption.override' | 'tech.fa.release' | 'fin.cost.approve'` (extensible string-union).
   - `ESignReceipt = { signatureId: string; signerUserId: string; intent: ESignIntent; subjectHash: string; signedAt: string; auditEventId: number; nonce: string }`.
   - `EReplayError`, `ESignSoDError`, `EPinFailedError`.
3. `packages/e-sign/src/sign.ts` — `signEvent(input): Promise<ESignReceipt>`:
   a. Validate input via Zod.
   b. Verify PIN via `verifyPin(pool, signerUserId, pin)` from `@monopilot/auth` — throws `EPinFailedError` on miss.
   c. Compute `subjectHash = sha256(canonical-json(input.subject))`.
   d. Check `e_sign_log` for existing row with same `(signer_user_id, subject_hash, intent, nonce)` — if present, throw `EReplayError`.
   e. INSERT row into `e_sign_log` (new table — see migration step) with `actor_user_id=signerUserId`, `intent`, `subject_hash`, `nonce`, `reason`, `created_at`.
   f. Write paired `audit_events` row with `action='e_sign.recorded'`, `retention_class='security'`, `before_state=null`, `after_state={intent, subjectHash, nonce}` — via existing audit API.
   g. Return `ESignReceipt`.
4. `packages/e-sign/src/dual.ts` — `dualSign(input): Promise<{ primary: ESignReceipt; secondary: ESignReceipt }>`:
   a. Reject if `primarySignerUserId === secondarySignerUserId` (`ESignSoDError`).
   b. Run `signEvent` for primary, then for secondary, both in a single tx; if secondary fails, ROLLBACK + rethrow.
5. New Drizzle migration `packages/db/migrations/0NNN-e-sign-log.sql`:
   ```
   CREATE TABLE e_sign_log (
     signature_id   UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
     org_id         UUID NOT NULL,
     signer_user_id UUID NOT NULL,
     intent         TEXT NOT NULL,
     subject_hash   TEXT NOT NULL,
     nonce          TEXT NOT NULL,
     reason         TEXT,
     created_at     TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
     UNIQUE (signer_user_id, subject_hash, intent, nonce)
   );
   ALTER TABLE e_sign_log ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
   ALTER TABLE e_sign_log FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
   CREATE POLICY e_sign_log_org_context ON e_sign_log
     USING (org_id = app.current_org_id())
     WITH CHECK (org_id = app.current_org_id());
   ```
6. `packages/e-sign/src/__tests__/sign.test.ts` (testcontainers Postgres):
   - Happy path: seed a user with a known PIN, call `signEvent`, assert receipt + e_sign_log row + audit_events row.
   - Replay: call `signEvent` twice with identical `{nonce, subject_hash}` — second throws `EReplayError`.
   - Wrong PIN: `signEvent` with bad PIN → throws `EPinFailedError`, no e_sign_log row written.
   - DualSign SoD: same-user primary + secondary → throws `ESignSoDError`, no rows written.
   - DualSign happy: two distinct users, both PINs correct → returns two receipts, two e_sign_log rows, two audit rows.

## Files in scope
- packages/e-sign/package.json [create]
- packages/e-sign/tsconfig.json [create]
- packages/e-sign/eslint.config.mjs [create]
- packages/e-sign/src/index.ts [create]
- packages/e-sign/src/types.ts [create]
- packages/e-sign/src/sign.ts [create]
- packages/e-sign/src/dual.ts [create]
- packages/e-sign/src/__tests__/sign.test.ts [create]
- packages/db/migrations/0NNN-e-sign-log.sql [create — verify NNN]
- packages/db/src/schema/e-sign.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/index.ts [modify — export e-sign]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a seeded user with known PIN, when signEvent({signerUserId, pin, intent:'qa.hold.release', subject:{holdId:'h-1'}, nonce:'n-1', reason:'ok'}) called, then receipt returned, one e_sign_log row with subject_hash = sha256(canonical-json({holdId:'h-1'})), one audit_events row action='e_sign.recorded' retention_class='security'.
2. Given previous successful signEvent, when SAME {signerUserId, intent, subject, nonce} replayed, then signEvent throws EReplayError and no new rows written.
3. Given wrong PIN, when signEvent called, then throws EPinFailedError, no e_sign_log row, no audit_events row.
4. Given dualSign called with primarySignerUserId === secondarySignerUserId, then throws ESignSoDError before any DB write; AND Given dualSign called with two distinct users and correct PINs, then returns two receipts; if secondary fails, ROLLBACK removes primary's row (verified by injecting faulty PIN on secondary).
## Test strategy
- RED: write `sign.test.ts` with the 5 ACs before implementation, using testcontainers Postgres + migration applied + a seeded test user (via `@monopilot/auth.setPin`). RED: `pnpm --filter @monopilot/e-sign test`.
- GREEN: implement migration + sign.ts + dual.ts + types.
- Run: `pnpm --filter @monopilot/db migrate && pnpm --filter @monopilot/e-sign test`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not store the PIN in plaintext anywhere — verification goes through `verifyPin` (argon2id).
- Do not skip the replay nonce — required for CFR 21 Part 11 non-repudiation.
- Do not write an `e_sign_log` row without a paired `audit_events` row in the same tx — atomicity is required.
- Do not allow the same user to be both signers in dualSign — SoD is the entire point.
- Do not return raw PIN or hashed PIN in any response field.
- Do not weaken `retention_class` from `security` — Part 11 §11.10(e) requires the full retention period.
- Do not expose this primitive from a public route handler without the rate-limit (T-121) wrapper — pin-verify preset applies.

pipeline_inputs.description: Foundation CFR 21 Part 11 e-sign primitive — signEvent + dualSign (SoD enforced) + server-verified PIN via @monopilot/auth + replay-nonce guard + e_sign_log RLS-isolated table + paired audit_events writes (retention_class='security'). Used by 09-Quality, 08-Production, 03-Technical, 10-Finance.
pipeline_inputs.details:
Zod-validated input. PIN check via existing verifyPin (argon2id, packages/auth T-016). subject_hash = sha256(canonical-json(subject)). UNIQUE(signer_user_id, subject_hash, intent, nonce) prevents replay. dualSign refuses same-user primary+secondary (ESignSoDError). All writes in single tx; rollback on any failure. Audit row retention_class=security (PRD §11 — 10y US tenants). Tests via testcontainers Postgres + seeded user with known PIN.

AC count consolidated from 5→4 by Fixer F7 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost).
out_of_scope: <ESignBlock> React component (09-Quality + 08-Production consumers — separate UI tasks)
Virtual PIN keypad component (BL-QA-06 — Quality UI task)

Given previous successful signEvent, when SAME {signerUserId, intent, subject, nonce} replayed, then signEvent throws EReplayError and no new rows written.
Given wrong PIN, when signEvent called, then throws EPinFailedError, no e_sign_log row, no audit_events row.

Given dualSign called with primarySignerUserId === secondarySignerUserId, then throws ESignSoDError before any DB write; AND Given dualSign called with two distinct users and correct PINs, then returns two receipts; if secondary fails, ROLLBACK removes primary's row (verified by injecting faulty PIN on secondary).

Do not skip replay nonce — Part 11 non-repudiation requirement.
Do not write e_sign_log row without paired audit_events row in same tx.
Do not allow same user as both signers in dualSign — SoD enforcement.
Do not return raw or hashed PIN in any response field.
Do not weaken retention_class from security — Part 11 §11.10(e).
Do not expose this primitive from public route handler without rate-limit (T-121) wrapper — pin-verify preset.
